'''Fire grenades''' or '''flame grenades''' were a special type of hand [[Grenade/Legends|grenade]] that released a quick burst of an incendiary agent which was very quick to ignite. The burns generally incapacitated the victims for a short while, but tough or well protected targets were known to ignore them. Such was the case with [[Jedi Sentinel/Legends|Jedi Sentinels]]<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'': [[Jedi Sentinel/Legends|Jedi Sentinels]] are immune to those effects.</ref> and [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] with many years of experience, like [[Canderous Ordo]], the first [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|leader of the Mandalorians]] following the [[Mandalorian Wars]].<ref name="Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords">''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'': ''Mandalorian Courage'' feature description.</ref>

A '''Special Fire Grenade''' was found by [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] in [[Tomb of Naga Sadow]] on [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], during his quest for the [[Star Forge]].

A cache of fire grenades were found on Tatooine when the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] was attempting to rescue [[Monarch of Naboo/Legends|Queen]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Amidala]].<ref name="SW:OW" />

==Behind the scenes==
In the [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'', the player can use the Special Fire Grenade on a pool of acid. The result would be death by noxious fumes. The grenades also appear in the [[2001]] video game [[Star Wars: Obi-Wan (video game)|''Star Wars: Obi-Wan'']].
